evaluations.query: Define a tool schema for a document search function
expected_behavior: Uses strict: true for schema validation
Sets additionalProperties: false to enforce strict schema compliance on tool inputs
All properties listed in required array
Includes clear 1-2 sentence description for each tool parameter and function

evaluations.query: Execute multiple LLM tool calls concurrently
expected_behavior: Uses asyncio.gather for parallel execution of independent tool calls concurrently
Disables parallel_tool_calls with strict mode to ensure sequential validation
Handles partial failures gracefully by returning results for successful calls alongside errors
Collects results from all tool calls

evaluations.query: Implement a tool execution loop with error handling
expected_behavior: Validates input parameters with Pydantic models before executing tool functions
Returns errors as tool results instead of crashing
Implements max iteration guard to prevent infinite tool-calling loops
Routes to appropriate tool by name

evaluations.query: Create a FastAPI SSE endpoint for streaming LLM responses
expected_behavior: Uses EventSourceResponse from sse-starlette for server-sent event streaming responses
Yields event/data pairs for each token
Sends done event on completion to signal the stream has finished
Uses async iterator for token generation

evaluations.query: Stream an LLM response that includes tool calls
expected_behavior: Accumulates tool call chunks by index
Separates content tokens from tool call data
Executes tools after full accumulation of all streamed tool call chunks
Handles multiple concurrent tool calls in stream

evaluations.query: Handle slow consumers in an LLM streaming pipeline
expected_behavior: Uses asyncio.Queue with maxsize for bounded buffer
Producer blocks when buffer is full
Consumer yields control with asyncio.sleep(0) to allow producer progress
Signals completion with None sentinel value to terminate the consumer loop

evaluations.query: Set up Ollama for local LLM inference in a Python project
expected_behavior: Installs Ollama via official script for local model inference setup
Pulls recommended models for task types
Configures environment variables for host and models
Integrates with LangChain via ChatOllama for standardized local model access

evaluations.query: Choose the right Ollama model for my hardware and task
expected_behavior: Matches model to hardware profile (M4 Max, M3 Pro, CI)
Recommends task-specific models (reasoning, coding, embeddings)
Covers quantization options (q4_K_M, q4_0) with quality and performance trade-offs
Includes VRAM requirements per model to match hardware capacity constraints

evaluations.query: Optimize Ollama performance on Apple Silicon for CI
expected_behavior: Sets num_ctx=32768 for Apple Silicon to maximize context window utilization
Uses keep_alive=5m for CI to avoid cold starts
Pre-warms models before first call to eliminate cold start latency
Implements provider factory for cloud/local switching

evaluations.query: Fine-tune a Llama model with QLoRA using Unsloth
expected_behavior: Loads model with load_in_4bit=True for QLoRA
Configures LoRA with r=16, alpha=32, dropout=0.05
Targets attention and MLP projection modules
Uses SFTTrainer from TRL for training

evaluations.query: Generate synthetic training data for fine-tuning
expected_behavior: Uses teacher model (GPT-5.2) to generate examples
Validates examples with separate validator model
Deduplicates using embedding similarity to remove semantically redundant training examples
Formats as Alpaca or ChatML template for consistent training data structure

evaluations.query: Align a fine-tuned model with DPO and evaluate it
expected_behavior: Uses DPOTrainer with preference dataset (chosen/rejected)
Sets beta=0.1 as KL penalty coefficient
Evaluates with LLM judge scoring to measure alignment quality objectively
Compares against baseline before deployment to verify fine-tuning improvement metrics

evaluations.query: My LLM calls are expensive because I load the entire knowledge base into every request. How do I reduce token costs with caching and budget allocation?
expected_behavior: Uses just-in-time document loading instead of pre-loading entire knowledge bases
Allocates a token budget per context section with percentage-based distribution
Selects compression strategy based on use case such as anchored iterative for long sessions
Validates compression quality with functional probes targeting ninety percent pass rate
Triggers compression at seventy percent utilization and targets fifty percent after compression

evaluations.query: My agent loses track of important information mid-conversation and the context window fills up with irrelevant tool outputs. How should I structure context?
expected_behavior: Positions critical information at start and end of context for high attention zones
Uses five-layer context architecture with defined allocation percentages per layer
Implements anchored iterative summarization preserving session intent and decisions made
Preserves last five messages uncompressed and compresses older history incrementally
Truncates tool outputs at source since they can consume over eighty percent of context

evaluations.query: I need to add quality gates to my LLM pipeline so bad outputs don't reach production. How do I evaluate model output quality with benchmarks?
expected_behavior: Implements quality gate with multi-metric assessment using a configurable threshold
Evaluates across multiple dimensions such as relevance accuracy and completeness
Runs batch evaluation over golden datasets with fifty or more samples for reliable metrics
Uses pairwise comparison with randomized ordering to eliminate position bias

evaluations.query: How do I set up LLM-as-judge evaluation metrics and detect hallucinations in RAG responses before they reach users?
expected_behavior: Uses a different model as judge rather than the model being evaluated
Evaluates across three to five dimensions with scores normalized to zero through one scale
Applies RAGAS metrics like faithfulness and context precision for RAG evaluation
Implements hallucination detection by checking output claims against provided context
Sets quality threshold at point seven for production and point six for drafts

evaluations.query: I need to design prompts for a complex reasoning task that requires step-by-step thinking. What prompt patterns should I use for chain-of-thought and few-shot examples?
expected_behavior: Implements chain-of-thought prompting with explicit step-by-step reasoning format
Uses three to five diverse representative examples for few-shot prompting
Orders few-shot examples with the most similar examples placed last for recency bias
Provides pattern selection guide matching task type to prompt pattern

evaluations.query: How do I version and A/B test my prompts in production to prevent silent regressions when prompts change?
expected_behavior: Uses Langfuse for prompt versioning with environment labels like production and staging
Tracks prompt variant via trace metadata for A/B comparison across versions
Applies DSPy MIPROv2 for automatic prompt optimization with Bayesian approach
Implements self-consistency with multiple reasoning paths and majority voting for hard problems

evaluations.query: My system prompts are verbose and I'm using the same expensive model for every task. How do I optimize prompts for token efficiency and cost?
expected_behavior: Removes redundant and verbose phrasing to reduce system prompt token count
Implements model tiering by task complexity routing simple tasks to cheaper models
Uses JSON mode with temperature zero for structured output consistency
Adds explicit constraints and negative examples to reduce hallucination rates
Maintains a prompt specification template with version and cost estimates

evaluations.query: I want to build an agent that reasons about which tools to use before calling them. How do I implement the ReAct thought-action-observation loop?
expected_behavior: Implements explicit Thought-Action-Observation loop with structured system prompt format
Caps iterations at five steps maximum to prevent runaway agent loops
Requires a Thought step before every Action to ensure reasoning before tool use
Parses Final Answer from response to terminate the loop when sufficient information gathered
Handles unknown tool names gracefully by returning error with available tool list

evaluations.query: My tool execution returns an empty string and the LLM enters an infinite loop calling the same tool repeatedly
expected_behavior: Returns a descriptive message like 'No results found' instead of an empty string from tool execution
Enforces a max iteration guard that terminates the tool loop after a bounded number of calls
Detects repeated identical tool calls and breaks the loop with an explanation to the LLM
Does not allow empty tool results to propagate back as they provide no signal for the model to reason about

evaluations.query: My SSE streaming endpoint leaks memory because the async generator keeps running after the client disconnects mid-stream
expected_behavior: Monitors the client disconnect signal via request.is_disconnected or abort controller in the streaming loop
Uses aclosing context manager to guarantee async generator cleanup when the connection is severed
Cancels any in-flight LLM API calls when the client disconnect is detected to stop token consumption
Does not rely on garbage collection to clean up long-running async generators after disconnect

evaluations.query: A user stores malicious instructions in a database record and when the search tool retrieves it the LLM follows the injected instructions instead of the system prompt
expected_behavior: Separates system instructions from tool output content using clear delimiters or role boundaries
Treats all tool output as untrusted user content and wraps it in explicit data-only markers
Does not concatenate raw tool results directly into the system prompt without sanitization
Implements output validation to detect when LLM behavior deviates from the system prompt after tool calls
